page: Microsoft Azure M416ds_12_v3 is an ultra-high-memory virtual machine belonging to the MDSHighMemoryv3 family. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Intel processors, this instance is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 416 dedicated vCPUs and a massive memory allocation of 11400.0 GB, which translates to approximately 27.4 GB of RAM per core.

The server includes a 410 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. It does not include any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile represents an extreme resource density, prioritizing massive memory capacity over local storage space or graphical acceleration.

Given the substantial memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, massive data warehousing, and complex enterprise resource planning systems. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a highly specialized tier where the investment is heavily weighted toward maximum RAM capacity and high-core-count compute density.
description: Microsoft Azure M416ds_12_v3 is an ultra-high-memory virtual machine from the MDSHighMemoryv3 family, designed for massive enterprise workloads. Powered by Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 416 dedicated vCPUs and an exceptional 11400.0 GB of RAM, yielding 27.4 GB of memory per core. The instance includes a 410 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This specialized hardware configuration is optimized for massive in-memory databases, large-scale data warehousing, and heavy enterprise resource planning applications where memory capacity is the primary bottleneck.
